content.description:
# Hob View Conversion Tracker

Tracking progress of converting JS views to Hob on the `hob-views` branch.

## Converted (15/41)

- sortable-list-view
- note-widget-view
- widget-markdown-view
- event-definition-view
- item-search-view
- error-view
- inline-card-view
- compact-card-view
- related-items-view
- element-inspector-view
- tag-browser-view
- error-list-view
- symbol-browser-view
- widget-slider-view *(first view using pageContext via invoke)*
- widget-radio-group-view

## Deferred — Needs Hob Primitives

All remaining unconverted views need one or more capabilities Hob doesn't have yet. Conversion is paused until the needed primitives are built.

| View | Lines | Blocking primitive(s) |
|------|------:|------|
| type-workshop-view | 750 | Persistent view state (mutable state across re-renders) |
| spatial-canvas-view | 617 | Sub-item rendering, spatial primitives, persistent state |
| system-browser-view | 575 | Persistent view state (multi-pane stateful UI) |
| viewport-view | 384 | Sub-item rendering, document events, browser history API |
| app-page-view | 374 | Sub-item rendering, view composition (pageContext), design mode |
| test-suite-view | 295 | Nested instance lifecycle, inter-frame messaging |
| hobson-instance-view | 147 | iframe creation, history.pushState monkey-patching |
| modal-frame-view | 75 | Sub-item rendering, DOM lifecycle (overlay add/remove), document events |
| kernel:default-view | — | System fallback, must stay JS until Hob is fully trusted |

## Skipped — JS-native

These views exist to execute user-specified JS code (`new Function(...)`) and would gain nothing from Hob conversion.

- widget-button-view — executes inline JS on click
- script-view — displays and runs JS code items
- widget-checkbox-group-view — executes user JS to load options, unused, poor design-mode UX

## Skipped — Trivial generic-view wrappers

~12 small views (2–10 lines) that just delegate to generic-view. Not worth converting — no logic to exercise.

## Strategy

The medium/widget frontier is complete. All remaining views are blocked on missing Hob primitives. The most impactful primitives to build next (unlocks the most views):

1. **Persistent view state** — unlocks type-workshop, system-browser (and simplifies existing converted views)
2. **Sub-item rendering** (`render-item` in Hob) — unlocks viewport, app-page, spatial-canvas, modal-frame
3. **Document-level events with cleanup** — unlocks viewport, modal-frame

Until these are built, further conversions would be mostly `invoke` calls — JS with extra parens — which defeats the purpose.
